decision: No ALiBi (and no ALiBi-style linear recency bias) in the 135M release model.
why: ALiBi lowers LM loss / extrapolates perplexity by making each head effectively a soft recency window — it suppresses distant tokens rather than using them. That trades away genuine long-range retrieval (long-file coding, long-document QA), which is exactly what the release model is for. Loss is a misleading selection metric here: ALiBi can win perplexity while losing the downstream/long-context comparison vs SmolLM2-135M.
reopen_if: A long-range eval (long-file code completion / needle-in-haystack retrieval) at our scale shows ALiBi >= RoPE on capability, not just loss.
refs: Kazemnejad et al. NeurIPS 2023 (positional encoding & length generalization)

decision: Long-context capability is a first-class, NON-NEGOTIABLE objective of the 135M release. REJECT any mechanism whose loss gain comes from suppressing or penalizing attention to distant tokens, even if it lowers val loss. Generalizes D001 from 'no ALiBi' to 'no distance-punishing attention'.
why: The release model's purpose (long-file coding, long-document QA/retrieval) needs genuine long-range attention. ALiBi, poly-ALiBi and KERPLE lower perplexity precisely BY turning each head into a soft recency window -- they suppress distant tokens instead of using them; hard sliding-window attention does the same by capping range outright. Val loss is therefore a MISLEADING selection metric here -- these win loss while destroying the capability we are building. Concretely, the tiny-tier champion's largest lever (alibi -> poly-alibi, lineage 175/230) is exactly such a mechanism, so its loss win does NOT transfer to the release objective; the poly-alibi ladder arm is CUT, leaving baseline + deepnet.
baseline: Full O(n^2) attention + RoPE (no distance penalty, full range).
rejects: hard sliding-window / local attention that caps reach
any monotonic distance penalty on pre-softmax scores
allows: RoPE + base/theta (NTK-aware) scaling for length generalization
sparse or selective attention that PRESERVES reach to distant tokens
reopen_if: A long-range capability eval (long-file code completion, needle-in-haystack retrieval, long-doc QA) at our scale shows the mechanism does NOT degrade long context vs RoPE full-attention -- loss alone never reopens it.
